questions.question: What is the output of: console.log(typeof null)?
questions.correctAnswer: B) object

questions.question: What is hoisting in JavaScript?
options: B) Variable and function declarations moved to top of scope before execution
C) Compiling code to machine language
questions.correctAnswer: B) Variable and function declarations moved to top of scope before execution

questions.question: What will this output: var x=1; function f(){ console.log(x); var x=2; } f();
questions.correctAnswer: C) undefined

questions.question: What is a closure?
options: A) A way to close browser tabs
B) Function that retains access to outer scope even after outer function returns
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function that retains access to outer scope even after outer function returns

questions.question: What is the temporal dead zone?
options: B) Period where let/const is declared but not yet initialized — accessing throws ReferenceError
C) Time between page load and script run
questions.correctAnswer: B) Period where let/const is declared but not yet initialized — accessing throws ReferenceError

questions.question: What does const guarantee in JavaScript?
options: B) Binding cannot be reassigned but object/array contents can still mutate
C) Creates an immutable deep-frozen object
D) Variable is deleted after block
questions.correctAnswer: B) Binding cannot be reassigned but object/array contents can still mutate

questions.question: What is the difference between == and ===?
options: B) == does type coercion before comparing; === checks value AND type strictly
questions.correctAnswer: B) == does type coercion before comparing; === checks value AND type strictly

questions.question: What does Object.freeze() do?
options: B) Prevents any property from being added, removed, or modified
C) Creates a deep immutable clone
questions.correctAnswer: B) Prevents any property from being added, removed, or modified

questions.question: What is the nullish coalescing operator (??) vs OR (||)?
options: B) ?? only triggers on null/undefined; || triggers on any falsy value (0, '', false too)
questions.correctAnswer: B) ?? only triggers on null/undefined; || triggers on any falsy value (0, '', false too)

questions.question: What is the output of: [1,2,3].reduce((acc,v) => acc+v, 10)?
What is a generator function?
options: B) Function that can pause and resume using yield keyword
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function that can pause and resume using yield keyword

questions.question: What does Symbol() create?
options: B) A unique, immutable primitive value guaranteed to never equal another Symbol
questions.correctAnswer: B) A unique, immutable primitive value guaranteed to never equal another Symbol

questions.question: What is the difference between let and var scope?
options: B) let is block-scoped {}; var is function-scoped
C) var is block-scoped; let is global
questions.correctAnswer: B) let is block-scoped {}; var is function-scoped

questions.question: What is optional chaining (?.) used for?
options: B) Safely accessing nested properties — returns undefined instead of throwing if intermediate is null/undefined
questions.correctAnswer: B) Safely accessing nested properties — returns undefined instead of throwing if intermediate is null/undefined

questions.question: What does the spread operator do with objects: const c = {...a, ...b}?
options: B) Creates shallow copy merging both objects — b properties override a on conflict
questions.correctAnswer: B) Creates shallow copy merging both objects — b properties override a on conflict

questions.question: What is WeakMap different from Map?
options: B) WeakMap keys must be objects and are weakly held — garbage collected when no other reference exists
D) WeakMap cannot be iterated (correct) but also keys are primitives
questions.correctAnswer: B) WeakMap keys must be objects and are weakly held — garbage collected when no other reference exists

questions.question: What is the output of: typeof class {}?
questions.correctAnswer: C) function

questions.question: What does Object.assign(target, source) do?
options: A) Deep clones source into target
B) Copies enumerable own properties from source to target — shallow copy
questions.correctAnswer: B) Copies enumerable own properties from source to target — shallow copy

questions.question: What is the purpose of IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression)?
options: B) Creates isolated scope to avoid polluting global namespace — executes immediately
questions.correctAnswer: B) Creates isolated scope to avoid polluting global namespace — executes immediately

questions.question: What is the event loop?
options: A) A for loop for DOM events
B) Mechanism that picks callbacks from queue and executes them when call stack is empty
questions.correctAnswer: B) Mechanism that picks callbacks from queue and executes them when call stack is empty

questions.question: Microtasks vs macrotasks — which runs first?
options: B) Microtasks (Promise.then, queueMicrotask) run before macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Microtasks (Promise.then, queueMicrotask) run before macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval)

questions.question: What is the output order: setTimeout(()=>console.log(1),0); Promise.resolve().then(()=>console.log(2)); console.log(3);
What does async/await compile to under the hood?
options: B) Syntactic sugar over Promises — async returns Promise, await pauses until Promise resolves
questions.correctAnswer: B) Syntactic sugar over Promises — async returns Promise, await pauses until Promise resolves

questions.question: What is Promise.allSettled() vs Promise.all()?
options: B) allSettled waits for ALL promises regardless; all rejects immediately on first rejection
questions.correctAnswer: B) allSettled waits for ALL promises regardless; all rejects immediately on first rejection

questions.question: What does Promise.race() return?
options: B) Settles with result of FIRST promise to settle — whether resolved or rejected
questions.correctAnswer: B) Settles with result of FIRST promise to settle — whether resolved or rejected

questions.question: How do you handle errors in async/await?
options: B) try/catch block around await calls
D) Errors cannot be caught in async
questions.correctAnswer: B) try/catch block around await calls

questions.question: What is AbortController used for in fetch?
options: B) Cancelling ongoing fetch requests by calling abort() on its signal
questions.correctAnswer: B) Cancelling ongoing fetch requests by calling abort() on its signal

questions.question: What is debouncing?
options: B) Delaying function execution until after specified time has passed since last invocation
C) Limiting function call frequency to once per interval
questions.correctAnswer: B) Delaying function execution until after specified time has passed since last invocation

questions.question: What is throttling vs debouncing?
options: B) Throttle ensures function runs at most once per interval; debounce delays until inactivity period ends
C) Debounce runs at fixed intervals
questions.correctAnswer: B) Throttle ensures function runs at most once per interval; debounce delays until inactivity period ends

questions.question: What is a Promise chain?
options: B) Sequence of .then() calls where each returns value passed to next handler
questions.correctAnswer: B) Sequence of .then() calls where each returns value passed to next handler

questions.question: What does fetch() return?
options: B) A Promise that resolves to Response object — need .json() or .text() to get body
questions.correctAnswer: B) A Promise that resolves to Response object — need .json() or .text() to get body

questions.question: What is the call stack?
options: A) Stack of function calls waiting to be made
B) Data structure tracking currently executing functions — LIFO, stack overflow if too deep
questions.correctAnswer: B) Data structure tracking currently executing functions — LIFO, stack overflow if too deep

questions.question: When does the callback queue get processed?
options: A) Immediately when callback is ready
B) Only when call stack is completely empty
questions.correctAnswer: B) Only when call stack is completely empty

questions.question: What is Promise.any() used for?
options: B) Resolves with value of FIRST fulfilled promise — ignores rejections unless ALL reject
questions.correctAnswer: B) Resolves with value of FIRST fulfilled promise — ignores rejections unless ALL reject

questions.question: Can you use await outside an async function?
options: B) Yes — at top level of ES modules (top-level await)
questions.correctAnswer: B) Yes — at top level of ES modules (top-level await)

questions.question: What does async function always return?
options: B) Always wraps return value in a Promise — even if you return a plain value
questions.correctAnswer: B) Always wraps return value in a Promise — even if you return a plain value

questions.question: What is the difference between callback hell and Promises?
options: B) Callbacks nest deeply (pyramid of doom); Promises chain flatly and handle errors better
questions.correctAnswer: B) Callbacks nest deeply (pyramid of doom); Promises chain flatly and handle errors better

questions.question: What happens if you throw inside an async function?
options: B) The returned Promise is rejected with that error
questions.correctAnswer: B) The returned Promise is rejected with that error

questions.question: What is queueMicrotask() used for?
options: B) Scheduling a microtask — runs after current task but before any macrotasks
C) Running tasks on separate thread
questions.correctAnswer: B) Scheduling a microtask — runs after current task but before any macrotasks

questions.question: What is prototypal inheritance?
options: B) Objects inheriting properties/methods from other objects through prototype chain lookup
questions.correctAnswer: B) Objects inheriting properties/methods from other objects through prototype chain lookup

questions.question: What does 'this' refer to in an arrow function?
options: B) Lexically inherited from enclosing scope — arrow functions have no own 'this'
questions.correctAnswer: B) Lexically inherited from enclosing scope — arrow functions have no own 'this'

questions.question: What does new keyword do?
options: B) Creates new object, sets __proto__ to constructor's prototype, binds this, returns object
questions.correctAnswer: B) Creates new object, sets __proto__ to constructor's prototype, binds this, returns object

questions.question: What is the difference between call() and bind()?
options: B) call() invokes function immediately with given this; bind() returns new function with bound this
D) call() only works with classes
questions.correctAnswer: B) call() invokes function immediately with given this; bind() returns new function with bound this

questions.question: What is memoization?
options: B) Optimization caching results of expensive function calls — same inputs return cached output
questions.correctAnswer: B) Optimization caching results of expensive function calls — same inputs return cached output

questions.question: What is currying?
options: B) Transforming f(a,b,c) into f(a)(b)(c) — each call takes one argument and returns function
questions.correctAnswer: B) Transforming f(a,b,c) into f(a)(b)(c) — each call takes one argument and returns function

questions.question: What is a pure function?
options: B) Always produces same output for same input AND has no side effects
C) Function with return statement only
questions.correctAnswer: B) Always produces same output for same input AND has no side effects

questions.question: What is the observer/pub-sub pattern?
options: B) Objects subscribe to events and get notified automatically when publisher emits event
questions.correctAnswer: B) Objects subscribe to events and get notified automatically when publisher emits event

questions.question: What is composition over inheritance?
options: B) Assembling complex behavior from small reusable functions instead of deep class hierarchies
questions.correctAnswer: B) Assembling complex behavior from small reusable functions instead of deep class hierarchies

questions.question: What does Object.create(proto) do?
options: B) Creates new object with proto as its __proto__ — inheritance without constructor call
questions.correctAnswer: B) Creates new object with proto as its __proto__ — inheritance without constructor call

questions.question: What is a Proxy object?
options: B) Wraps target object intercepting fundamental operations like get, set, delete via handlers
questions.correctAnswer: B) Wraps target object intercepting fundamental operations like get, set, delete via handlers

questions.question: What is function composition?
options: B) Combining f and g to create h where h(x) = f(g(x)) — output of one is input of next
questions.correctAnswer: B) Combining f and g to create h where h(x) = f(g(x)) — output of one is input of next

questions.question: What is the singleton pattern?
options: B) Design pattern ensuring only ONE instance of class exists — returns same instance every time
questions.correctAnswer: B) Design pattern ensuring only ONE instance of class exists — returns same instance every time

questions.question: What is the factory pattern?
options: B) Function that creates and returns objects without using 'new' — centralizes object creation
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function that creates and returns objects without using 'new' — centralizes object creation

questions.question: What does class syntax in JS compile to?
options: B) Syntactic sugar over constructor functions and prototype-based inheritance
questions.correctAnswer: B) Syntactic sugar over constructor functions and prototype-based inheritance

questions.question: What is Symbol.iterator?
options: B) Well-known symbol defining custom iteration — makes object compatible with for...of
questions.correctAnswer: B) Well-known symbol defining custom iteration — makes object compatible with for...of

questions.question: What is the strategy pattern used for?
options: B) Encapsulating interchangeable algorithms — select algorithm at runtime without changing client code
questions.correctAnswer: B) Encapsulating interchangeable algorithms — select algorithm at runtime without changing client code

questions.question: What is tail call optimization (TCO)?
options: B) Reusing current stack frame for recursive call in tail position — prevents stack overflow
questions.correctAnswer: B) Reusing current stack frame for recursive call in tail position — prevents stack overflow

questions.question: What is the module pattern vs ES6 modules?
options: B) Module pattern uses IIFE for privacy; ES6 modules use import/export with file-level scope
C) ES6 modules use IIFE too
questions.correctAnswer: B) Module pattern uses IIFE for privacy; ES6 modules use import/export with file-level scope

questions.question: What is the decorator pattern?
options: B) Dynamically adding behavior to objects/functions without modifying original — wraps with new functionality
questions.correctAnswer: B) Dynamically adding behavior to objects/functions without modifying original — wraps with new functionality

questions.question: Which keyword is used to declare constant in JavaScript?
questions.correctAnswer: C) const

questions.question: Which symbol is used for comments in JS?
Which operator compares value and type in JS?
